failure_modes.text: False helplessness gives away agency by treating influenceable problems as if they are wholly external.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the classification: ask what specific action the team could take to influence the outcome; if an action exists, the item is not purely external.

failure_modes.text: Control illusion mistakes activity for leverage and assumes the team can directly move a system mostly constrained by external actors or feedback loops.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish influence from direct control: focus effort on the highest-leverage moves available rather than assuming the team can force outcomes in constrained systems.

failure_modes.text: Static categorization locks items into one bucket and never revisits the classification after new data or changed incentives.
failure_modes.mitigation: Revisit the categorization when new data arrives or incentives change; static buckets decay as the environment shifts.

premortem_questions.text: Which item did we mark uncontrollable without evidence?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "Which item did we mark uncontrollable without evidence?"

premortem_questions.text: Where are we assuming control we do not actually have?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "Where are we assuming control we do not actually have?"

premortem_questions.text: What one action would increase our influence on the highest-impact external factor?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What one action would increase our influence on the highest-impact external factor?"

heuristics.text: Split the situation into direct control, indirect influence, and no-control buckets before generating actions.
heuristics.source_quote: split them into three buckets: direct control, indirect influence, and no control

heuristics.text: Create actions only for the control and influence buckets and monitoring rules for the rest.
heuristics.source_quote: Create actions only for the first two buckets and monitoring rules for the third.

heuristics.text: Reclassify the buckets when new information or incentives change what is influenceable.
heuristics.source_quote: what was uncontrollable yesterday may become influenceable after new information or changed incentives

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on agency misclassification and bucket-based action design, so the chunk set stays close to those moves.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style mindset coaching overlays because the raw source is stronger on explicit boundary mistakes and operational mitigation.
open_questions: The third heuristic is lightly normalized from the latticework section rather than from a standalone imperative line.

allies: The raw latticework section points cleanly toward first-principles-thinking and risk-vs-uncertainty as later relation material.
antagonists: The source is lighter on named antagonists but does preserve tension with systems-thinking and game-theory style adaptive contexts.
structured_tensions: There is no explicit structured tension block, so any later tension preservation should stay conservative and review-first.
